subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: execute build pipeline (if configuration present)

subcommands.name: compile
subcommands.description: compile contract source files

options.description: list all recent stable releases from solc-bin. If filter is specified then it will display only that type of release or docker tags

value.name: filter
value.description: the filter parameter must be one of the following: prereleases, releases, latestRelease or docker

options.description: compile all contracts instead of only the contracts changed since last compile
suppress all compilation output

subcommands.name: config
subcommands.description: set user-level configuration options

options.description: enable Truffle to send usage data to Google Analytics
disable Truffle's ability to send usage data to Google Analytics

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: get a Truffle configuration option value

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: set a Truffle configuration option value

subcommands.name: console
subcommands.description: run a console with contract abstractions and commands available

options.description: specify the network to use

value.name: name
value.description: network name must exist in the configuration

options.description: log communication between Truffle and the Ethereum client
preload console environment from required JavaScript file

value.name: file
value.description: the default export must be an object with named keys that will be used to populate the console environment. For example, if your JavaScript is module.exports =

options.description: do not load any user-defined JavaScript into the console environment. This option takes precedence over --require, -r, and values provided for console.require i

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: helper to create new contracts, migrations and tests

states.name: artifact_type
states.description: create a new artifact where artifact_type is one of the following: contract, migration, test or all. The new artifact is created along with one(or all) of the f

subcommands.name: debug
subcommands.description: interactively debug any transaction on the blockchain

options.description: the network to connect to
allows the debugger to download source from source verification services to debug transactions involving external contracts. When used, a transaction hash is re
allows the debugger to compile Solidity test contracts. Implies --compile-all
forces the debugger to recompile all contracts, even when it would otherwise judge doing so unnecessary. Compilation results are not saved
skip recompilation even when it may be needed

states.name: transaction_hash
states.description: transaction ID to use for debugging. You can omit this to simply start the debugger and then load a transaction later

subcommands.name: deploy
subcommands.description: alias for migrate. See migrate for details

subcommands.name: develop
subcommands.description: open a console with a development blockchain

options.description: start/Connect to a Truffle develop session and log all RPC activity. See the Log RPC Activity docs for more information about using this option
preload console environment from required JavaScript file

value.name: file
value.description: the default export must be an object with named keys that will be used to populate the console environment. For example, if your JavaScript is module.exports =

options.description: do not load any user-defined JavaScript into the console environment. This option takes precedence over --require, -r, and values provided for console.require i

subcommands.name: exec
subcommands.description: execute a JS module within the Truffle environment

options.description: specify the network to use, using artifacts specific to that network

value.name: name
value.description: network name must exist in the configuration

options.description: compile contracts before executing the script

states.name: script.js
states.description: javaScript file to be executed. Can include path information if the script does not exist in the current directory. (required)

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: display a list of all commands or information about a specific command

states.name: command
states.description: display usage information about the specified command

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: initialize new and empty Ethereum project

options.description: initialize project regardless of the current working directory's state. Be careful, this could overwrite existing files that have name conflicts

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: install a package from the Ethereum Package Registry

states.name: package_name
states.description: name of the package as listed in the Ethereum Package Registry. (required)

states.name: @version
states.description: when specified, will install a specific version of the package, otherwise will install the latest version

subcommands.name: networks
subcommands.description: show addresses for deployed contracts on each network

options.description: remove all network artifacts that aren't associated with a named network

subcommands.name: obtain
subcommands.description: fetch and cache a specified compiler

options.description: download and cache a version of the solc compiler. (required)

subcommands.name: opcode
subcommands.description: print the compiled opcodes for a given contract

states.name: contract_name
states.description: name of the contract to print opcodes for. Must be a contract name, not a file name. (required)

subcommands.name: publish
subcommands.description: publish a package to the Ethereum Package Registry

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: run a third-party plugin command

states.name: command
states.description: name of a command defined by an installed plugin. (required)

subcommands.name: version
subcommands.description: show version number and exit

subcommands.name: watch
subcommands.description: watch filesystem for changes and rebuild the project automatically

subcommands.name: preserve
subcommands.description: save data to decentralized storage platforms like IPFS and Filecoin

options.description: environment name, as defined in truffle-config `environments` object
preserve to IPFS
preserve to Filecoin
preserve to Textile Buckets

subcommands.name: migrate
subcommands.description: run migrations to deploy contracts

options.description: run all migrations from the beginning, instead of running from the last completed migration
run contracts from a specific migration

value.name: number
value.description: the number refers to the prefix of the migration file

options.description: run contracts to a specific migration

value.name: number
value.description: the number refers to the prefix of the migration file

options.description: specify the network to use, saving artifacts specific to that network

value.name: name
value.description: network name must exist in the configuration

options.description: compile all contracts instead of intelligently choosing which contracts need to be compiled
log communication between Truffle and the Ethereum client
fork the network specified and only perform a test migration
skip the test migration performed before the real migration
prompt to confirm that the user wants to proceed after the dry run
prints additional status messages

subcommands.name: unbox
subcommands.description: download a Truffle Box, a pre-built Truffle project

options.description: unbox project in the current directory regardless of its state. Be careful, this will potentially overwrite files that exist in the directory

subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: run JavaScript and Solidity tests

options.description: compile all contracts instead of intelligently choosing which contracts need to be compiled
like --compile-all, but compiles contracts in debug mode for extra information. Has no effect on Solidity <0.6.3
specify the network to use, using artifacts specific to that network

value.name: name
value.description: network name must exist in the configuration

options.description: log communication between Truffle and the Ethereum client
log all contract events
provides global debug() function for in-test debugging. Usable with Javascript tests only; implies --compile-all
allows one to rename the debug() function to something else
bail after the first test failure. May be abbreviated -b
enable mixed JavaScript and Solidity stack traces
shortcut for --stacktrace --compile-all-debug

states.name: test_file
states.description: name of the test file to be run. Can include path information if the file does not exist in the current directory
